mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, causing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust domestic demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in >55% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a high-inflation regime reflects supply constraints and margin compression, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during high CPI periods do not predict negative 20-day returns or Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp monthly inflation spikes trigger immediate market repricing of future monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in >60% of high-inflation-spike instances despite the threshold crossing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirms a strengthening labor market that supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarterly growth in real GDP reflects expanding economic output and corporate revenue potential, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 growth remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive 60-day returns occur in <45% of expansion quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Three consecutive months of industrial production contraction signals a manufacturing downturn that reduces corporate profit expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX yields positive 20-day returns in >65% of instances following three-month industrial production declines.
